126778.explanation: We want liveliness in our centers. If there is a need for extended opening hours, we are open to that. The intention is that this promotes cooperation among entrepreneurs and does not lead to additional nuisance for residents.

126779.explanation: Weert Lokaal supports these initiatives but does not see a direct need to financially contribute as a government. The municipality can support these initiatives in other ways.

126780.explanation: Absolutely, these do not fit into the landscape and economy of Weert.

126782.explanation: Weert Lokaal acknowledges concerns about health risks from using wood stoves but currently sees no need to ban them in Weert. Many things are already prohibited in the Netherlands. We advocate for conscious use, good information, and clear rules to limit health risks.

126784.explanation: Weert Lokaal values safety but also finds it important that the tradition of fireworks and social gatherings on New Year's Eve continues.

126785.explanation: Weert Lokaal believes residents should have the option to dispose of bulky waste locally.

126788.explanation: This is already done by sports funds. Physical activity is important for everyone. Weert Lokaal believes it is important that youth and motivated adults stay active. It is important for the individual and ultimately means lower healthcare costs and thus costs for society.

126789.explanation: The local broadcaster is already funded by the government. Weert Lokaal finds the local broadcaster important to provide independent and reliable information to our residents, including younger generations.

126794.explanation: Weert Lokaal believes more and better enforcement is needed in the city center, villages, and neighborhoods for the greatest nuisances experienced by our residents and entrepreneurs.

126795.explanation: Where possible, camera surveillance can support enforcement and has a preventive effect.

126797.explanation: Community enforcement officers (BOAs) should be given more tools to enable enforcement. Also, to feel safe while enforcing rules.

126800.explanation: In a tight housing market, speculation must be combated. Every resident of Weert deserves the chance for suitable housing in Weert.

126801.explanation: Weert Lokaal supports the transformation of buildings when possible. However, this can be done without subsidies from the municipality, leaving it to the market for office buildings. In cases of social objectives, the municipality can contribute to the unprofitable part.

126802.explanation: This already happens in Weert. People on social assistance are encouraged to participate according to their abilities. Participation can take various forms such as day activities, volunteering, or education to eventually move towards paid employment.

126804.explanation: Recently, the municipality raised income limits so that working people can also benefit from the schemes. Recent research shows that the current income limits used by Weert municipality are socially fair and realistic.

126806.explanation: In the municipality of Weert, there are currently enough charging points in neighborhoods, districts, and villages. Expansion is only needed if there is demand. When a resident requests a charging station, we respond accordingly.

126807.explanation: Weert Lokaal finds traffic safety important, especially at busy locations near schools and community buildings. Almost everywhere, the speed limit is already 30 km/h. Main roads could remain 50 km/h. 30 km/h would mean many investments and congestion in neighborhoods and villages.

126810.explanation: If this care provider is available and contracted, a resident can always choose.

126812.explanation: There are already various distribution points in Weert for tampons and sanitary pads to support residents with low incomes and to break the taboo.

126815.explanation: Business parks may expand within our economic profile: for local SMEs, technical manufacturing industry, and NOT for unrelated logistics (boxes).

126817.explanation: Newcomers also need housing but should not be given priority. Local residents are also on the waiting list and deserve allocation of a home and a fair chance. Weert Lokaal favors a fair and humane system. We want to eliminate waiting lists.

126819.explanation: Agreed, unless the land is also used for other purposes such as agricultural use. We welcome dual use of land. Ideally, solar panels should be on roofs, and there is still plenty of space for that.

126822.explanation: Municipality of Weert allocates funds for Swim2Play, in which every primary school in Weert participates. It is important that young people can move freely and safely in the water.

126823.explanation: Weert Lokaal focuses on preserving culture and heritage, which includes the dialect. Our Limburg language is part of that. The curriculum in schools is primarily the responsibility of the schools themselves, with support from the municipality/province.

126824.explanation: The municipality of Weert has 2 coffeeshops. This is sufficient.

126826.explanation: Weert Lokaal prefers to build upwards rather than at the expense of green spaces and meeting areas in neighborhoods and villages.

126827.explanation: Weert Lokaal believes renting out single rooms harms livable neighborhoods and villages. The housing stock should primarily remain available for starters, families, and through-flow. For other groups, we choose suitable and well-regulated solutions.

126830.explanation: The parking balance for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors must be correct. For this, we look at more parking options. Bicycle parking is also important. We want to continue encouraging residents who can to travel to the city center by bike, on foot, or public transport.

126832.explanation: Weert Lokaal always puts the health of our residents first.

126778.explanation: The municipality should only set rules if things get out of hand.

126779.explanation: These residents receive support, but that does not necessarily have to be financial. Support can be considered case by case.

126780.explanation: Many online purchases require distribution centers, but these centers should NOT be located in residential areas or the city center, but close to the A2 motorway.

126782.explanation: In Weert, wood burning is not a major problem regarding air quality; the municipality should only intervene when there is nuisance.

126784.explanation: With the national fireworks ban for consumers, the municipality should regulate this tradition professionally, centrally, and safely.

126785.explanation: Waste collection is a core task of the municipality. Collection of bulky waste can be organized, for example, once a month via neighborhood and village councils.

126788.explanation: The municipality can contribute but not pay the full membership fee.

126789.explanation: The municipality must keep up with the times and carefully consider where residents get their information from (internet, TV, radio, or local papers) and allocate funds proportionally.

126794.explanation: Visible neighborhood teams that are easily approachable increase the sense of safety.

126795.explanation: Smart cameras enhance security and deter wrongdoers.

126797.explanation: And pepper spray, so the municipal enforcement officers (boa's) also gain more authority.

126800.explanation: Anyone who buys a home should be free to decide whether to live in it or rent it out.

126801.explanation: The owner can receive cooperation from the municipality to realize housing, but the owner must manage on their own.

126802.explanation: Social assistance is a safety net, not a hammock. Those who receive assistance should give something back to society.

126804.explanation: Recent research shows that 1 in 8 residents struggles to make ends meet. The municipality must take action to improve this.

126806.explanation: Charging is already possible in many places and the driving range keeps increasing. It is unnecessary to reserve parking spaces exclusively for electric cars.

126807.explanation: In many neighborhoods the speed limit is already 30 km/h but enforcement is lacking. No 30 km/h on ring roads and main connecting roads, but yes on the ring canals.

126810.explanation: The freedom of choice lies with the people who need care. The municipality has no role in this.

126812.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to distribute contraceptives. Organizations that do can possibly apply for subsidies.

126815.explanation: Do not expand existing sites; a new site near the A2 is possible, for example on Roermondseweg.

126817.explanation: Status holders can register and simply participate in the system, without priority.

126819.explanation: Solar panels on roofs come first; there are still enough suitable roofs. Also on business premises, for example.

126822.explanation: It is good for children to learn to swim; the municipality may subsidize this.

126823.explanation: The municipality should not impose an obligation to speak only Dutch.

126824.explanation: But the permit should be revoked in case of nuisance.

126826.explanation: High-rise buildings are necessary to facilitate housing needs, but they must fit the scale of Weert and the planned location in the area.

126827.explanation: The current rules are clear enough, and permits must be revoked in case of nuisance.

126830.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces, but the municipality should not reduce these as has happened in the past.

126832.explanation: The municipality should do what it can to prioritize public health over economic interests.

126778.explanation: The Weert VVD supports more room for entrepreneurship. More freedom in opening hours is acceptable provided entrepreneurs take responsibility to prevent disproportionate nuisance to the surroundings.

126779.explanation: The Weert VVD supports the generation of local sustainable energy but believes the municipality should not subsidize such initiatives, as this distorts market forces and does not belong to core municipal tasks.

126780.explanation: Due to scarce space, the Weert VVD wants to be selective in the allocation of new business plots: local companies and those fitting Weert’s economic profile (technical manufacturing industry).

126782.explanation: The Weert VVD believes the municipality should impose as few additional rules as possible. Use of open fireplaces and wood stoves can sometimes cause nuisance but we also highly value residents’ individual freedom.

126784.explanation: The Weert VVD does not consider organizing a fireworks show on New Year’s Eve a core municipal task. However, the municipality should cooperate in facilitating such an event if initiated by the community.

126785.explanation: The Weert VVD believes costs for municipal services should be fairly charged to residents: the user pays. Collecting bulky waste via the recycling center is an efficient system that keeps residents’ expenses as low as possible.

126788.explanation: The Weert VVD sees sports as essential for a healthy and resilient society. Financial support exists for children who cannot participate due to their parents' financial situation. Supporting adults financially is not considered a municipal task.

126789.explanation: The Weert VVD values an independent local broadcaster. The municipality receives funds from the national government, which we consider sufficient to provide good local broadcasting in Weert.

126794.explanation: The Weert VVD advocates more enforcement and a tough approach to nuisance. Therefore, it is necessary to further increase the capacity of municipal enforcement officers (boa's) so enforcement can take place in more areas of the city.

126795.explanation: The Weert VVD believes everyone should feel safe in Weert. We want to invest in visible and effective measures, such as targeted camera surveillance in places where nuisance or insecurity is experienced.

126797.explanation: The Weert VVD supports more enforcement and a tough approach to nuisance. If needed, the use of batons should be among the available options.

126800.explanation: The Weert VVD is not in favor of additional municipal regulations on the housing market. This undermines property rights and hinders free market operation.

126801.explanation: The Weert VVD believes the market should transform office buildings into housing. There have already been good examples in Weert, such as the former UWV office on Emmasingel/Maaspoort.

126802.explanation: The Weert VVD wants everyone who can participate to do so. Working, caregiving, entrepreneurship, volunteering, or contributing to the neighborhood: participation keeps our society vital and engaged. Therefore, we support a social contribution in return for those receiving social assistance.

126804.explanation: The Weert VVD believes work is the best form of social security. We focus strongly on reintegration and participation. Raising income limits for poverty aid reduces the incentive to work more, which we consider undesirable.

126806.explanation: The Weert VVD supports good accessibility. Cars remain welcome everywhere in our view. We focus on sufficient parking facilities, whether you drive electric or not. New charging points for electric cars should not come at the expense of regular parking spaces.

126807.explanation: The Weert VVD advocates good accessibility, smooth traffic flow, and more traffic safety. Reducing speed limits is a good measure in some places, but not everywhere. Speed must suit the road design and surroundings.

126810.explanation: The Weert VVD believes everyone deserves good care. Freedom of choice is very important, but the growing demand for care requires smart choices to keep care affordable and accessible in the future.

126812.explanation: The Weert VVD believes that the municipality should focus on its core tasks. Providing free menstrual products is not a core task of the municipality. In Weert, this is done by private initiatives such as the Food Bank.

126815.explanation: The Weert VVD supports more space for entrepreneurship. We advocate the expansion of the Weert Boulevard and the development of a new modern business park further along the Roermondseweg. This way, we can relocate existing companies and attract new ones.

126817.explanation: The Weert VVD opposes giving priority to status holders for social rental housing. This is unfair compared to other housing applicants who have been registered for social housing for years.

126819.explanation: The Weert VVD supports smart dual use of space, so that as little scarce space as possible is used for the generation and storage of sustainable energy. There is still enough roof surface available in our municipality to install solar panels.

126822.explanation: The Weert VVD believes the municipality should focus on its core tasks. Parents primarily have the responsibility to ensure their children learn to swim.

126823.explanation: The Weert VVD believes the municipality should not interfere with the curriculum of our primary schools because this is not a municipal task.

126824.explanation: The Weert VVD does not support establishing a new coffeeshop. There is no reason to open an additional coffeeshop in the municipality alongside the two currently existing ones.

126826.explanation: The Weert VVD wants more houses built quickly in Weert, both within the city and on its outskirts. For example, in the Spoorzone, we see room for 'high-rise' buildings (up to 15 floors), provided they fit well into the surroundings.

126827.explanation: The Weert VVD supports room rental, provided it does not cause nuisance to the neighborhood. We also want to prevent too much room rental in one area. Therefore, we support a maximum of 10 percent per postal code area.

126830.explanation: The Weert VVD is in favor of good accessibility. We believe cars should be welcome everywhere. We aim for more public parking facilities so that our city center remains easily accessible for residents and visitors in the future.

126832.explanation: The Weert VVD does not support regulations on pesticides that go beyond legal requirements. Furthermore, the municipality does not have the capacity or means to enforce such rules.

126778.explanation: The current regulation is already generous. From the perspective of preventing nuisance, it is undesirable to make it even more lenient.

126779.explanation: The municipality can assist residents in developing projects and may provide temporary financial support.

126780.explanation: The CDA has strongly opposed the darkening in recent years.

126782.explanation: There is no problem that justifies a ban. It is the users' responsibility to prevent nuisance to neighbors.

126785.explanation: There is a trial with a pickup service. We want to wait for the results before deciding if and how to organize this for the entire municipality. Nothing is free. If someone brings something free of charge to the recycling center, we all pay for it through the waste tax.

126788.explanation: There are already several schemes in Weert that provide financial support for low-income people, for example for such a membership.

126789.explanation: The local broadcaster receives funds from the national government. We do not see the need for additional funding from the municipality.

126794.explanation: Supervisors should be deployed more on real problems and be more visible.

126795.explanation: There is no reason for that.

126797.explanation: The community enforcement officers (boa's) are not police officers and should not become so.

126800.explanation: This requirement already applies to new homes. We certainly want to keep it that way. A new housing regulation is being prepared. We want to examine whether including an obligation for owner-occupation when purchasing an existing home is necessary and possible.

126801.explanation: Possibly, but only if it resolves a deteriorated situation and the municipality requires that social rental housing be created in the building.

126802.explanation: Such an obligation exists. A regulation for this is included in the municipality's reintegration ordinance.

126804.explanation: Since January 1st, the individual income allowance applies to everyone with an income up to 110% of the social assistance standard. Previously, this was 100%. More people are now eligible. We supported the proposal to raise this income limit.

126806.explanation: Designating charging spots must not come at the expense of the number of general parking spaces. In many residential streets, parking spaces are already scarce.

126807.explanation: On roads within the built-up area - not the 30 km/h zones - 50 km/h is an appropriate speed.

126810.explanation: If someone wants something very specific, they must arrange it themselves. This is not a municipal task.

126812.explanation: The schools and the Sponsorwinkel (Sponsor Shop) already do this.

126815.explanation: There is a plan for a new business park on the Roermondseweg (between the railway and Roermondseweg). That is sufficient. If opportunities arise to convert business parks in urban areas into housing, we want to seize them. Expansion of business parks is impossible due to lack of space.

126817.explanation: We do not want a priority position over other groups who are equally in need of housing.

126819.explanation: We want to maximize the use of roof capacity. Solar panels on agricultural land reduce farmland and nature and cause landscape pollution.

126822.explanation: The existing Swim2Play is a good replacement for the old school swimming lessons.

126823.explanation: We attach great importance to folk culture. The dialect is part of that.

126824.explanation: There may be two coffeeshops in Weert. That is sufficient.

126826.explanation: A height of 4-5 floors is acceptable in certain urban situations. We do not completely rule out real high-rise buildings, but we are very critical of them. There must also be demand for such housing. We should not build them just because a developer wants to.

126827.explanation: The risk of nuisance to the neighborhood is too great.

126830.explanation: There are currently enough parking spaces. If new functions arise that require more parking, such as housing, sufficient additional spaces must be created.

126832.explanation: The municipality does not have the authority for this.

126778.explanation: Whether it is responsible to allow a hospitality venue to determine its own opening hours depends entirely on the circumstances. It makes a difference whether the establishment is located in a residential area or in the city center. This is therefore considered on a case-by-case basis.

126779.explanation: The local generation of sustainable energy is one of the main points in our program. The municipality must take a leading role in this. Residents should also have more say in this, all within reasonable limits, of course.

126780.explanation: We are against the construction of new block-shaped buildings and prefer to focus on manufacturing industries.

126782.explanation: We want to discourage wood burning due to its negative effects on people with respiratory problems. We primarily focus on good information provision about this before considering a ban.

126784.explanation: Now that the commercial sale of fireworks is prohibited, the municipality can offer a good alternative for this, preferably in cooperation with local hospitality businesses.

126785.explanation: Weert has a well-functioning waste collection system. Citizens can already dispose of their bulky waste for free.

126788.explanation: There are already various arrangements for this within the municipality of Weert. We do not want to cut back on these and will expand them where necessary.

126789.explanation: WeertFM is important to the local community. The municipality should therefore continue to support it.

126794.explanation: Safety in the municipality must be guaranteed. For that, we need enforcement officers (boa’s), but boa’s alone are not enough. Neighborhood design and social facilities also play a role here.

126795.explanation: Camera surveillance is acceptable where it is necessary and meaningful.

126797.explanation: In principle, boas should be able to defend themselves. A condition for batons is that boas are properly trained in how to handle them.

126800.explanation: Homes are for living in, not for making money. We have been advocating for years for a regulation to formalize this.

126801.explanation: Converting existing buildings is one of the ways to quickly create housing.

126802.explanation: Most people on welfare are open to this and can do valuable community work. Using this instrument as a punishment helps no one. We must consider the best solution for each individual. We have successfully applied this approach in Weert for years.

126804.explanation: GL-PvdA Weert has advocated for years for raising the standard for certain benefits to 130% of the legal minimum. This goal is supported by a report from the FNV.

126806.explanation: More electric cars are being sold, so there is increased demand for charging stations. However, sufficient parking spaces must remain available for everyone.

126807.explanation: This is an important step in making Weert’s traffic safer and causes hardly any time loss for motorists.

126810.explanation: Freedom of choice is important. However, the healthcare provider must be reliable and competent.

126812.explanation: This already happens, for example at our secondary schools.

126815.explanation: The municipality is looking for a new business park. However, GL-PvdA Weert does not want block-shaped distribution centers there.

126817.explanation: It is a legal obligation for the municipality to provide housing for refugees. The shortage of homes can only be solved by building more.

126819.explanation: First of all, we want to install solar panels on buildings, parking lots, etc. Only in special circumstances should it be possible to place them on agricultural land.

126822.explanation: It is important that every child learns to swim. This must be maintained throughout the entire school period.

126823.explanation: We advocate more attention for the Weerter and Royer dialects. Attention at primary schools is one part of this.

126824.explanation: At present, the existing structure of coffeeshops functions properly. There is no question of establishing a new one.

126826.explanation: As far as we are concerned, residential towers may be built in Weert, but not higher than the church tower.

126827.explanation: This must be allowed to tackle the housing shortage. However, the rented rooms must be of sufficient quality.

126830.explanation: The number of parking spaces must basically meet the demand. We aim to increasingly house parking underground.

126832.explanation: GL-PvdA Weert wants a ban on discharging PFAS substances and harmful pesticides to protect the health of residents. Pesticides that have no negative health effects will remain permitted.

126778.explanation: D66 wants an attractive city with sufficient nightlife options. This may seem appealing, but from a safety and nuisance perspective, it cannot be allowed.

126779.explanation: D66 considers such initiatives important and wants to support them, for example through a subsidy scheme. Laws and regulations are leading in this.

126780.explanation: D66 generally supports this wish. It is important to tailor our procedures accordingly.

126782.explanation: D66 believes we should not regulate more than necessary. As long as no nuisance is experienced, D66 considers regulation unnecessary.

126784.explanation: D66 thinks a fireworks show will lead to meeting and connection. Moreover, it is an attractive alternative to the fireworks ban.

126785.explanation: D66 finds the development from 'waste to raw material' very important. This requires good processing and that costs money. Free disposal at the waste station can be an incentive.

126788.explanation: D66 believes everyone should be able to participate. This also applies to participating in sports and culture. Within our current poverty policy, sufficient financial space must remain available for this.

126789.explanation: D66 finds a local broadcaster very important. This local broadcaster is already financially supported. Currently, extra money is not needed.

126794.explanation: D66 does not believe that extra penalties are the best measure to reduce nuisance. However, D66 recognizes that people’s (traffic) behavior increasingly leads to problems, also in Weert. More enforcement officers (boa’s) can be a measure, but more is needed.

126795.explanation: D66 is not in favor of camera surveillance. However, in certain places it can be an effective measure. Limited and specific use of camera surveillance can be appropriate. This is already applied at events.

126797.explanation: The law does not allow this. Furthermore, it has been shown to cause more escalation, which also endangers the personal safety of the enforcement officers (boa’s). Weapons are appropriate for the police.

126800.explanation: This is already government policy and D66 wants to maintain it in Weert.

126801.explanation: D66 wants vacant buildings to receive a new function. If converting them into housing is possible, D66 wants to encourage this. This doesn't always have to be through subsidies, but can also be through cooperation in development planning.

126802.explanation: D66 is a strong advocate for participation because everyone should be able to join in! That provides a sense of self-worth. However, D66 is not in favor of a universally applicable measure for everyone. Every case should be considered individually to see what is possible!

126804.explanation: D66 believes that good assistance should be accessible! Therefore, a general measure is undesirable. It is not income that determines this, but the disposable amount. This is already municipal policy. D66 currently sees no reason to change this.

126806.explanation: D66 considers sustainability a priority. When electric driving increases or can be stimulated by more charging stations, D66 certainly wants to support this! However, the driving range of new cars is increasing so rapidly that this must be taken into account.

126807.explanation: This general measure is encouraged by the national government. D66 supports this development. It should only be applied where possible. Traffic must also be able to quickly enter and leave the city.

126810.explanation: D66 considers diversity and freedom extremely important. Everyone may be who they want to be. Our diversity & inclusion policy must respond to this as much as possible. However, it is not always possible to have suitable caregivers, doctors, and so forth.

126812.explanation: D66 also considers a general measure undesirable in this respect.

126815.explanation: D66 finds it very important that Weert is attractive to live and work in! Weert is a real manufacturing city with good, beautiful industry. D66 wants to maintain this. Limited growth and innovation are needed for this.

126817.explanation: D66 wants to continue using the distribution set out in the dispersal law as a starting point. The established national policy indicates how many refugees Weert must accommodate.

126819.explanation: D66 considers the energy transition very important. In addition, laws and regulations for farmers are becoming increasingly complex. There is also a retirement scheme. This sometimes makes energy production an attractive alternative for farmers and the municipality. Here too, D66 does not want a general measure.

126822.explanation: D66 believes that the municipality already does this, among other things by maintaining the swimming pool. It is important that residents can swim due to all the water in the municipality.

126823.explanation: D66 attaches great value to the Limburg identity, including the dialect. It is up to the schools themselves to decide how they want to give this form. D66 wants the municipality to continue stimulating this by supporting initiatives.

126824.explanation: D66 believes there is sufficient supply.

126826.explanation: Weert continues to develop. D66 sees high-rise buildings as both necessary for affordability and desirable for the number of homes to be built. However, D66 wants to maintain the currently applicable maximum height of 15 residential floors.

126827.explanation: There is also a housing shortage in Weert. Especially when it comes to housing temporary employees, room rental is often chosen. D66 wants to allow this only to a limited extent to prevent undesirable effects.

126830.explanation: D66 believes the center must remain easily accessible. At the same time, D66 wants to encourage residents to leave their cars behind more often. For this, D66 wants fast and cheap public transport from the suburbs to the center.

126832.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to make clear agreements with farmers about the use of pesticides and good alternatives and to record these agreements in a covenant that can be enforced. Preferably together with other municipalities and agricultural organizations.

126778.explanation: OnsWeert supports regulation within the applicable municipal regulations.

126779.explanation: OnsWeert wholeheartedly supports citizen initiatives but does not consider financial support from the municipality to be conditional.

126780.explanation: New distribution centers do not contribute to improved livability for the municipality and its residents.

126782.explanation: OnsWeert follows the municipality's advice to always first check the Heating Guide (Stookwijzer).

126784.explanation: Since there is a national fireworks ban from this year, OnsWeert supports a central (fireworks) show on New Year's Eve.

126785.explanation: If bulky waste is collected free of charge at home, this will lead to increased costs for residents. The environmental station is a good alternative for this.

126788.explanation: OnsWeert finds it important that children can participate well in sports and exercise. There are national funds available for people receiving social assistance benefits. The municipality has a facilitating role here.

126789.explanation: With the current subsidy, the municipality emphasizes the importance of the local broadcaster. Additional funding is not necessary.

126794.explanation: OnsWeert considers more enforcement officers (boa's) and thus more safety on the streets important.

126795.explanation: OnsWeert supports more camera surveillance at high-risk locations.

126797.explanation: Enforcement officers (boa's) should be better able to defend themselves in this increasingly hardened society.

126800.explanation: OnsWeert wants to prevent homes from being bought on a large scale by external parties for renting. Private investment in a home for a family member or rental should remain possible.

126801.explanation: OnsWeert believes we should be cautious with subsidies, but the housing market need is high, so measures such as subsidies to convert vacant offices into housing are necessary.

126802.explanation: Where the situation allows, people receiving social assistance should do socially meaningful work in return. This also promotes social involvement and increases chances on the labor market.

126804.explanation: Raising the income limit for aid with poverty is open for discussion with OnsWeert if this does not directly mean financial support is provided. It is important that people just above the poverty line who have a problem can turn to the municipality for help in the form of support.

126806.explanation: Designating more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars should not cause or worsen parking problems. The municipality must consider growth if there is demand.

126807.explanation: Reducing the entire built-up area to 30 km/h is not a solution. Where traffic safety is at risk, a suitable solution can be considered. OnsWeert also finds enforcement around schools important.

126810.explanation: OnsWeert's principle is that people should be able to get good and accessible care. However, the shortage of caregivers makes it unfeasible to choose a caregiver based on religion, culture, or belief.

126812.explanation: OnsWeert recognizes that period poverty is a problem.

126815.explanation: Expansion of an industrial estate is possible for Weert's manufacturing industry and not for further urbanization. This can also create space in urban areas, for example for housing. Prioritize repurposing vacancies and prevent fertile soil from disappearing.

126817.explanation: OnsWeert finds it important that Weert residents are given priority for social housing.

126819.explanation: There are many good alternatives to solar panels on agricultural land, such as solar on roofs, noise barriers, or covering parking lots.

126822.explanation: OnsWeert supports school swimming because it offers multiple benefits such as exercise, water safety, and social interaction.

126823.explanation: OnsWeert sees attention to Limburg and Weert culture as a great added value if this is made possible in all our primary schools.

126824.explanation: OnsWeert believes the current number of coffeeshops is sufficient.

126826.explanation: OnsWeert wants to preserve the rural character of Weert. According to us, high-rise buildings mainly belong within the ring roads.

126827.explanation: OnsWeert opposes renting out houses in separate rooms, but supports house subdivision.

126830.explanation: It is important that there remain sufficient parking options for visitors and residents in the center. OnsWeert wants the number of parking spaces to meet parking standards.

126832.explanation: The use of pesticides is regulated by national and European policies. OnsWeert does not see the need to impose additional local rules on this.

statements.title: The municipality should tighten the rules for the use of pesticides at nurseries.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de regels voor het gebruik van

titleParts.glossary: Substances used to control weeds, insects or diseases.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
In Weert there should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOA’s) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: In Weert there should be more CCTV surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Limburgish language.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: In Weert tall apartment blocks should be allowed.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: In Weert business parks should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The BOA’s who inspect safety in Weert should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Weert controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: In Weert the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Weert moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.
